steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb of the second independent clause: 'they ____________ valuable items,' The first clause uses simple past ('merchants exported'), so the blank needs a finite verb in matching tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — to obtain: non-finite; cannot serve as a main verb in an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice B — obtained: simple past, finite — matches the tense of 'exported' and correctly anchors the second clause. ✓
  • Choice C — obtaining: non-finite; creates a dangling modifier, not a main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — having obtained: non-finite; cannot stand as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
That is the correct answer.
